text: Continuous integration is a software development practice where developers frequently merge their code changes into a shared repository. Each integration is verified by an automated build and running tests. This process is managed through a CI pipeline, which automatically compiles the code and executes a suite of tests every time new code is pushed. By running tests early and often, teams can catch issues immediately, preventing small problems from growing into bigger bugs later. This leads to more stable software and faster development cycles overall.
